input.prompt:
I have a .NET class library at src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj that I want to publish
to nuget.org using trusted publishing (OIDC) instead of an API key.
The repo is hosted on GitHub at myorg/mylib. Help me set this up.

data.content: There are two parts to this: **nuget.org configuration** (manual) and a **GitHub Actions workflow** (I'll create). Let me set up the workflow and project file.
data.reasoningText:
The directory is empty. I need to create the project structure and the GitHub Actions workflow. Let me also check for any existing files.

The user said they have a .NET class library at `src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj`. Let me create the necessary files:

1. The `.csproj` file with NuGet package metadata
2. A GitHub Actions workflow for publishing using trusted publishing (OIDC)

For NuGet trusted publishing (OIDC), the key components are:
1. On nuget.org: Configure the package to trust the GitHub repository/workflow
2. In GitHub Actions: Use `dotnet nuget push` with `--source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json` and the OIDC token


3. The workflow needs `permissions: id-token: write` to obtain the OIDC token from the `actions/id-token` action

I'll set up the `.csproj` file with package metadata and create the GitHub Actions workflow that handles the trusted publishing flow. Setting up the dotnet environment and packing the NuGet package, then using `dotnet nuget push` to publish it. For the OIDC flow, I need to request a token from GitHub Actions and exchange it with nuget.org for a temporary API key to authenticate the push.

Creating the project structure and workflow...

arguments.file_text:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>

    <!-- NuGet package metadata -->
    <PackageId>MyLib</PackageId>
    <Version>1.0.0</Version>
    <Authors>myorg</Authors>
    <Description>MyLib class library</Description>
    <PackageLicenseExpression>MIT</PackageLicenseExpression>
    <PackageProjectUrl>https://github.com/myorg/mylib</PackageProjectUrl>
    <RepositoryUrl>https://github.com/myorg/mylib</RepositoryUrl>
    <RepositoryType>git</RepositoryType>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

name: Publish to NuGet (Trusted Publishing)

on:
  release:
    types: [published]

# Restrict default permissions; grant only what's needed.
permissions: {}

jobs:
  publish:
    name: Pack & Publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    # The "nuget" environment gates the deployment and must match what you
    # register on nuget.org as the trusted-publisher environment.
    environment: nuget

    permissions:
      id-token: write   # Required to request the OIDC token
      contents: read     # Required to check out the repo

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: 8.0.x

      - name: Restore
        run: dotnet restore src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj

      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj --configuration Release --no-restore

      - name: Pack
        run: dotnet pack src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj --configuration Release --no-build --output ./nupkgs

      # Exchange the GitHub OIDC token for a short-lived NuGet API key.
      - name: Get NuGet trusted-publishing token
        id: nuget-tp
        run: |
          oidc_token=$(curl -sS -H "Authorization: bearer $ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN" \
            "${ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL}&audience=api://AzureADTokenExchange" | jq -r '.value')

          api_key=$(curl -sS -X POST \
            https://api.nuget.org/v3/trusted-publishers/tokens \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"token\":\"${oidc_token}\"}" | jq -r '.')

          echo "::add-mask::${api_key}"
          echo "api-key=${api_key}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Push to NuGet.org
        run: |
          dotnet nuget push ./nupkgs/*.nupkg \
            --source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json \
            --api-key "${{ steps.nuget-tp.outputs.api-key }}"

toolArgs.file_text:
name: Publish to NuGet (Trusted Publishing)

on:
  release:
    types: [published]

# Restrict default permissions; grant only what's needed.
permissions: {}

jobs:
  publish:
    name: Pack & Publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    # The "nuget" environment gates the deployment and must match what you
    # register on nuget.org as the trusted-publisher environment.
    environment: nuget

    permissions:
      id-token: write   # Required to request the OIDC token
      contents: read     # Required to check out the repo

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: 8.0.x

      - name: Restore
        run: dotnet restore src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj

      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj --configuration Release --no-restore

      - name: Pack
        run: dotnet pack src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj --configuration Release --no-build --output ./nupkgs

      # Exchange the GitHub OIDC token for a short-lived NuGet API key.
      - name: Get NuGet trusted-publishing token
        id: nuget-tp
        run: |
          oidc_token=$(curl -sS -H "Authorization: bearer $ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_TOKEN" \
            "${ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL}&audience=api://AzureADTokenExchange" | jq -r '.value')

          api_key=$(curl -sS -X POST \
            https://api.nuget.org/v3/trusted-publishers/tokens \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"token\":\"${oidc_token}\"}" | jq -r '.')

          echo "::add-mask::${api_key}"
          echo "api-key=${api_key}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Push to NuGet.org
        run: |
          dotnet nuget push ./nupkgs/*.nupkg \
            --source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json \
            --api-key "${{ steps.nuget-tp.outputs.api-key }}"

data.content:
Here's what I created and what you still need to do:

### Created files

1. **`src/MyLib/MyLib.csproj`** — added NuGet package metadata (`PackageId`, `Version`, etc.). Adjust as needed.
2. **`.github/workflows/publish.yml`** — triggers on GitHub Release, then:
   - Builds & packs the library
   - Exchanges a GitHub OIDC token for a short-lived NuGet API key via `api.nuget.org/v3/trusted-publishers/tokens`
   - Pushes the `.nupkg`

The workflow uses a **`nuget` environment** and requests `id-token: write` permission.

### Manual steps on nuget.org

1. Go to **nuget.org → Manage Packages → MyLib → Trusted Publishers** (or set it up during the first push via "manage trusted publishers" on your account).
2. Add a trusted publisher with:
   - **Repository owner:** `myorg`
   - **Repository:** `mylib`
   - **Workflow:** `publish.yml`
   - **Environment:** `nuget`
3. In your GitHub repo, create a **deployment environment** named `nuget` under **Settings → Environments** (optionally add reviewers for gated releases).

After that, creating a GitHub Release will trigger the workflow and publish without any API key secrets.
